name: Masters athlete cutting weight while preserving muscle
description: 48-year-old in a calorie deficit, wants to lose body fat without losing strength. Recovery capacity is reduced (age + deficit). Plan should keep heavy intensity to preserve muscle but limit total volume, with 2:1 loading instead of 3:1.
must_have: Heavy compound work preserved (e.g. 3-5 reps RPE 7-8 on main lifts) — preserves strength in a cut
2:1 loading (2 loading weeks + 1 deload), not 3:1 — masters athlete in deficit has reduced recovery
Total weekly working sets capped around 12-15 per muscle (not 18-20) given reduced recovery
Optional light cycling at Z2 for cardiovascular health — short sessions only (≤30 min)
Deload week clearly cuts volume by ≥30%
must_not_have: Adding HIIT or intense intervals — interferes with strength recovery during deficit
Very high-rep failure training (>15 reps to failure) on every set — counterproductive in cut for muscle preservation
Squats or deadlifts on consecutive days
Long endurance sessions (>45 min) — would compromise the strength priority in a deficit
